messages.content:
Add a --sandboxed cli argument that, when supplied, will start a docker container at the beginning of the conversation and tear it down afterwards. The docker image used should be "alpine", and the container should mount the current directory to /workspace (which should also be the workdir inside the container).

When invoking the shell_execute tool, it should perform a "docker exec" inside this container rather than performing it on the host.
I'll help you add a `--sandboxed` CLI argument that runs commands in a Docker container. Let me first explore the project structure to understand the codebase.

tools.parameters:
{"path": "src/cli.ts", "edits": [{"search":"    const disableToolsFlags = '--disable-tools <string>'
    const disableToolsDescription = 'Comma-separated list of tool names to disable.'

    program
        .option(historyFlags, historyDescription)
        .option(portFlags, portDescription)
        .option(cwdFlags, cwdDescription)
        .option(yoloFlags, yoloDescription)
        .option(oneShotFlags, oneShotDescription)
        .option(disableToolsFlags, disableToolsDescription)
        .action(options => {
            if (options.cwd) {
                process.chdir(options.cwd)
            }

            chat(
                preferences,
                rules,
                providers,
                embeddingsClients,
                tracker,
                options.history,
                options.port,
                options.oneShot,
                options.yolo,
                options.disableTools,
            )
        })","replacement":"    const disableToolsFlags = '--disable-tools <string>'
    const disableToolsDescription = 'Comma-separated list of tool names to disable.'

    const sandboxedFlags = '--sandboxed'
    const sandboxedDescription = 'Run shell commands in a Docker container (alpine image).'

    program
        .option(historyFlags, historyDescription)
        .option(portFlags, portDescription)
        .option(cwdFlags, cwdDescription)
        .option(yoloFlags, yoloDescription)
        .option(oneShotFlags, oneShotDescription)
        .option(disableToolsFlags, disableToolsDescription)
        .option(sandboxedFlags, sandboxedDescription)
        .action(options => {
            if (options.cwd) {
                process.chdir(options.cwd)
            }

            chat(
                preferences,
                rules,
                providers,
                embeddingsClients,
                tracker,
                options.history,
                options.port,
                options.oneShot,
                options.yolo,
                options.disableTools,
                options.sandboxed,
            )
        })"}]}

messages.content: Now I need to modify the shell_execute tool to use docker exec when sandboxed. Let me check the shell execution utility to understand how commands are currently executed.
Now I need to modify the shell execution to use Docker when sandboxed. I'll update the `runCommand` function to check for the Docker container and use it accordingly.

tools.name: edit_file
tools.parameters: {"path": "src/util/shell/exec.ts", "edits": "[{\"search\": \"async function runCommand(context: ChatContext, command: string, update: Updater<OutputLine[]>): Promise<OutputLine[]> {\\n    return context.interruptHandler.withInterruptHandler(signal => {\\n        return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\\n            const output: OutputLine[] = []\\n            const aggregate = (type: 'stdout' | 'stderr', s: string) => {\\n                if (!signal.aborted) {\\n                    output.push({ content: s, type })\\n                    update(output)\\n                }\\n            }\\n            const shellCommand = context.preferences.shellCommand ?? 'zsh'\\n            const cmd = spawn(shellCommand, ['-c', command])\\n            cmd.stdout.on('data', data => aggregate('stdout', data.toString()))\\n            cmd.stderr.on('data', data => aggregate('stderr', data.toString()))\\n\\n            signal.addEventListener('abort', () => treeKill(cmd.pid!, 'SIGKILL'))\\n\\n            cmd.on('exit', exitCode => {\\n                if (exitCode === 0 && !signal.aborted) {\\n                    resolve(output)\\n                } else {\\n                    reject(new Error(`exit code ${exitCode}`))\\n                }\\n            })\\n        })\\n    })\\n}\", \"replacement\": \"async function runCommand(context: ChatContext, command: string, update: Updater<OutputLine[]>): Promise<OutputLine[]> {\\n    // Use Docker container if sandboxed mode is enabled\\n    if (context.dockerContainer?.isRunning()) {\\n        return runDockerCommand(context, command, update)\\n    }\\n\\n    return context.interruptHandler.withInterruptHandler(signal => {\\n        return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\\n            const output: OutputLine[] = []\\n            const aggregate = (type: 'stdout' | 'stderr', s: string) => {\\n                if (!signal.aborted) {\\n                    output.push({ content: s, type })\\n                    update(output)\\n                }\\n            }\\n            const shellCommand = context.preferences.shellCommand ?? 'zsh'\\n            const cmd = spawn(shellCommand, ['-c', command])\\n            cmd.stdout.on('data', data => aggregate('stdout', data.toString()))\\n            cmd.stderr.on('data', data => aggregate('stderr', data.toString()))\\n\\n            signal.addEventListener('abort', () => treeKill(cmd.pid!, 'SIGKILL'))\\n\\n            cmd.on('exit', exitCode => {\\n                if (exitCode === 0 && !signal.aborted) {\\n                    resolve(output)\\n                } else {\\n                    reject(new Error(`exit code ${exitCode}`))\\n                }\\n            })\\n        })\\n    })\\n}\\n\\nasync function runDockerCommand(context: ChatContext, command: string, update: Updater<OutputLine[]>): Promise<OutputLine[]> {\\n    return context.interruptHandler.withInterruptHandler(signal => {\\n        return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\\n            const output: OutputLine[] = []\\n            const aggregate = (type: 'stdout' | 'stderr', s: string) => {\\n                if (!signal.aborted) {\\n                    output.push({ content: s, type })\\n                    update(output)\\n                }\\n            }\\n\\n            const containerId = context.dockerContainer!.getContainerId()!\\n            const cmd = spawn('docker', ['exec', containerId, 'sh', '-c', command])\\n            cmd.stdout.on('data', data => aggregate('stdout', data.toString()))\\n            cmd.stderr.on('data', data => aggregate('stderr', data.toString()))\\n\\n            signal.addEventListener('abort', () => treeKill(cmd.pid!, 'SIGKILL'))\\n\\n            cmd.on('exit', exitCode => {\\n                if (exitCode === 0 && !signal.aborted) {\\n                    resolve(output)\\n                } else {\\n                    reject(new Error(`exit code ${exitCode}`))\\n                }\\n            })\\n        })\\n    })\\n}\"\"}]"}

'zsh'\\n            const cmd = spawn(shellCommand, ['-c', command])\\n            cmd.stdout.on('data', data => aggregate('stdout', data.toString()))\\n            cmd.stderr.on('data', data => aggregate('stderr', data.toString()))\\n\\n            signal.addEventListener('abort', () => treeKill(cmd.pid!, 'SIGKILL'))\\n\\n            cmd.on('exit', exitCode => {\\n                if (exitCode === 0 && !signal.aborted) {\\n                    resolve(output)\\n                } else {\\n                    reject(new Error(`exit code ${exitCode}`))\\n                }\\n            })\\n        })\\n    })\\n}\", \"replacement\": \"async function runCommand(context: ChatContext, command: string, update: Updater<OutputLine[]>): Promise<OutputLine[]> {\\n    // Use Docker container if sandboxed mode is enabled\\n    if (context.dockerContainer?.isRunning()) {\\n        return runDockerCommand(context, command, update)\\n    }\\n\\n    return context.interruptHandler.withInterruptHandler(signal => {\\n        return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\\n            const output: OutputLine[] = []\\n            const aggregate = (type: 'stdout' | 'stderr', s: string) => {\\n                if (!signal.aborted) {\\n                    output.push({ content: s, type })\\n                    update(output)\\n                }\\n            }\\n            const shellCommand = context.preferences.shellCommand ?? 'zsh'\\n            const cmd = spawn(shellCommand, ['-c', command])\\n            cmd.stdout.on('data', data => aggregate('stdout', data.toString()))\\n            cmd.stderr.on('data', data => aggregate('stderr', data.toString()))\\n\\n            signal.addEventListener('abort', () => treeKill(cmd.pid!, 'SIGKILL'))\\n\\n            cmd.on('exit', exitCode => {\\n                if (exitCode === 0 && !signal.aborted) {\\n                    resolve(output)\\n                } else {\\n                    reject(new Error(`exit code ${exitCode}`))\\n                }\\n            })\\n        })\\n    })\\n}\\n\\nasync function runDockerCommand(context: ChatContext, command: string, update: Updater<OutputLine[]>): Promise<OutputLine[]> {\\n    return context.interruptHandler.withInterruptHandler(signal => {\\n        return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {\\n            const output: OutputLine[] = []\\n            const aggregate = (type: 'stdout' | 'stderr', s: string) => {\\n                if (!signal.aborted) {\\n                    output.push({ content: s, type })\\n                    update(output)\\n                }\\n            }\\n\\n            const containerId = context.dockerContainer!.getContainerId()!\\n            const cmd = spawn('docker', ['exec', containerId, 'sh', '-c', command])\\n            cmd.stdout.on('data', data => aggregate('stdout', data.toString()))\\n            cmd.stderr.on('data', data => aggregate('stderr', data.toString()))\\n\\n            signal.addEventListener('abort', () => treeKill(cmd.pid!, 'SIGKILL'))\\n\\n            cmd.on('exit', exitCode => {\\n                if (exitCode === 0 && !signal.aborted) {\\n                    resolve(output)\\n                } else {\\n                    reject(new Error(`exit code ${exitCode}`))\\n                }\\n            })\\n        })\\n    })\\n}\"\"}]"}
